# TransLoc3D : Point Cloud based Large-scale Place Recognition using Adaptive Receptive Fields

- **Work ID:** `LPR-071`
- **Reviewed version:** `arxiv:2105.11605`
- **Year / venue / type:** 2021 / arXiv / `arxiv_preprint`
- **Curation tier:** `evidence_reviewed`
- **Relevance:** `core_lpr`
- **Primary method:** [Voxel / sparse learning](../landscape.md#branch-learning-based-voxel-sparse)
- **Links:** [Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.11605) · [Reviewed official code](https://github.com/slothfulxtx/TransLoc3D)

## Accepted structured facts

- **datasets:** Oxford RobotCar, Business District (B.D.), Residential Area (R.A.), University Sector (U.S.) — `source_fact`; manual, p. 9–11, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-VI
- **input_unit:** single ground-removed point-cloud submap downsampled to 4096 points and scaled to \[-1,1\] — `source_fact`; manual, p. 9–10, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-VI
- **invariances:** point-order permutation, no claimed rigid-pose invariance — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 9–10, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-VI
- **representation:** sparse voxelization and 3D sparse convolutions, five-branch adaptive receptive-field module, point-wise feature reweighting, external-attention transformer, NetVLAD with context gating, 256-D global descriptor, triplet margin loss with batch-hard negative mining — `source_fact`; manual, p. 3–9, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-VI
- **uses_learning:** yes — `source_fact`; manual, p. 1–18, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-VI
- **uses_sequence:** no — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–18, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-VI
